name: Zafra - running circular through the historic quarter and the Via de la Plata
name_original: Zafra – running circular por el casco histórico y la Vía de la Plata
region_intro: Jogging routes around Zafra traverse a landscape characterized by gentle hills and open plains, typical of the Extremadura region in Spain. The area features a mix of historic urban environments, such as the old town, and rural paths that follow ancient routes like the Vía de la Plata. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making the terrain accessible for various running abilities. Best jogging routes around Zafra The most popular jogging route is Zafra - running circular through the historic quarter and the Via de la Plata, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This route offers a blend of urban exploration and historical pathways. Another top favourite among local runners is Arco Del Cubo – Arco de San Antonio loop from Zafra, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This running route features a mix of town and surrounding natural areas, with an elevation gain of over 120 meters. Local runners also love the Zafra Old Town – Arco Del Cubo loop from Zafra Feria, an 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through the historic Zafra Old Town and its periphery, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Zafra is defined by historic urban paths, sections of the Vía de la Plata, and surrounding rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to more challenging running trails. The routes in Zafra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Zafra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Zafra?
A: There are over 35 running routes available around Zafra, offering a variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 300 times.

Q: Are there easy running routes in Zafra suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, Zafra offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Zafra&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 4.9 km long with moderate elevation changes, making it a comfortable introduction to running in the area.

Q: Where can I find longer running trails in Zafra?
A: For those seeking longer distances, Zafra has routes that extend further into the surrounding landscape. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31453911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafra Old Town – Arco Del Cubo loop from Zafra Feria&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 13.5 km and takes you through the historic town and its periphery.

Q: Are there challenging running trails around Zafra?
A: While Zafra's terrain is generally characterized by gentle hills, some routes offer more significant elevation gains for a challenging run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco Del Cubo – Arco de San Antonio loop from Zafra&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features an elevation gain of over 120 meters across its 9.7 km, providing a good workout.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on Zafra's running routes?
A: Zafra's running routes offer a diverse experience, blending historic urban environments with rural paths. You'll encounter the charm of the old town, sections of ancient routes like the Vía de la Plata, and open plains typical of the Extremadura region. Many routes combine both town exploration and natural areas.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see while running in Zafra?
A: Many routes in Zafra pass by significant historical sites. You might run past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parador Nacional Duques de Feria&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3804712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Tower of Zafra&lt;/a&gt;, or the vibrant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6458193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Grande, Zafra&lt;/a&gt;. These offer glimpses into the region's rich heritage.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Zafra?
A: The komoot community highly rates Zafra's running routes, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Runners often praise the unique blend of urban exploration through the historic quarter and the opportunity to run along ancient pathways like the Vía de la Plata, appreciating the accessible terrain and varied scenery.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Zafra?
A: Yes, many of Zafra's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992972207/zafra-running-circular-through-the-historic-quarter-and-the-via-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafra - running circular through the historic quarter and the Via de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a convenient loop through key areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Zafra?
A: Given Zafra's location in Extremadura, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running. Summers can be quite hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable during those months. Winters are typically mild.

Q: Are the running trails in Zafra generally dog-friendly?
A: Many of the rural paths and sections of the Vía de la Plata around Zafra are suitable for running with dogs, especially if they are kept on a leash. When running through the historic town, be mindful of other pedestrians and local regulations. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

Q: Are there running routes in Zafra that are suitable for families?
A: Yes, several routes are suitable for families, especially those looking for an easy and scenic jog. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Zafra&lt;/a&gt; is a good option, offering a relatively flat and manageable distance that can be enjoyed by various age groups.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer viewpoints in Zafra?
A: While Zafra is characterized by gentle hills rather than dramatic mountains, some routes provide elevated sections that offer pleasant views of the town and the surrounding Extremaduran plains. The blend of urban and rural paths often allows for changing perspectives as you run.
tips:
The Parador de Zafra is located in what was once the palace of the Dukes of Feria. The building is a clear example of its long and rich history, which includes the discoverer from Extremadura, Hernán Cortés, an illustrious guest of the palace.
The Parador preserves beautiful coffered ceilings, chests, ironwork, handrails and decorative elements from the old palace.

The tower of the convent of San Francisco is the only remains of what was the Franciscan convent of San Benito. It was located outside the city of Zafra, although it is currently within the urban area.
The convent was founded and built in the last third of the 15th century by the second Count of Feria, Gomes II Suárez de Figueroa, and by his mother, Countess María Manuel. Starting in 1565, the cloister and bedrooms were enlarged with the aim of housing a greater number of religious.
